text.title: Spring Boot Layered Architecture Failure Modes
text.summary: 4 antipatterns observed in real Spring Boot deployments; each pairs symptom + root cause + concrete fix.

antipattern.symptom: LazyInitializationException in JSON response; works in tests, fails at runtime.
antipattern.root-cause: Entity serialized outside @Transactional scope; lazy associations fetched after session close.
antipattern.fix: Convert entity to DTO inside the service while @Transactional active; use @EntityGraph for required associations.

antipattern.symptom: List endpoint runs N+1 queries; latency scales linearly with result size.
antipattern.fix: Use `@EntityGraph(attributePaths=...)` on the repository method, or JOIN FETCH in JPQL, or projection DTOs via constructor query.

antipattern.symptom: Transaction held across HTTP write; long-running requests block connection pool.
antipattern.root-cause: @Transactional placed on controller method — tx scope = entire HTTP exchange.
antipattern.fix: Move @Transactional to the @Service method; controller stays HTTP-only.

antipattern.symptom: Unit test fails because `@Autowired` field is null; requires Spring container to test trivially.
antipattern.root-cause: Field injection makes the class untestable without the container.
antipattern.fix: Refactor to constructor injection (final fields); test with plain `new Service(mockRepo)`.
